sections.conclusion: In the usual residential situation, Government Code §87206.5 excludes the principal residence itself and the lot or real property on which the principal residence is located from disclosure, so long as the property does not constitute any other type of reportable interest such as a reportable investment or reportable income. When the real property consists of land in addition to the lot on which the principal residence sits, all such additional property is disclosable.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a filer's principal residence and the lot on which it is located must be disclosed under new Government Code §87206.5, which exempts principal residences from real property disclosure requirements.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that a principal residence and its lot are excluded from disclosure under Government Code §87206.5, but any additional real property beyond the residential lot must be disclosed if it would otherwise be reportable.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is internal FPPC memorandum with distribution list rather than traditional letter format. No formal 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' headers present, but content provides clear policy interpretation. Image quality affects confidence - some text is unclear or partially obscured. Parsed as advice letter based on FPPC providing interpretive guidance.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a filer's principal residence and the lot on which it is located must be disclosed under new Government Code §87206.5, which exempts principal residences from real property disclosure requirements.

CONCLUSION: In the usual residential situation, Government Code §87206.5 excludes the principal residence itself and the lot or real property on which the principal residence is located from disclosure, so long as the property does not constitute any other type of reportable interest such as a reportable investment or reportable income. When the real property consists of land in addition to the lot on which the principal residence sits, all such additional property is disclosable.

At the opinion request meeting on December I 1976, it was agreed that in the usual residenti f this Code section excludes the principal residence i f and the lot or real property on which the principal residence is located, so long as the property does not constitute any other type of reportable interest, such as a report- able investment or reportable income. embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum providing policy interpretation of Government Code §87206.5 regarding which real property interests must be disclosed, clarifying that principal residences and their lots are excluded from disclosure requirements.
